subtasks.title: Remove .codex/ files from Git tracking

artifact_quality: git ls-files .codex/ returns empty output (zero tracked files)
.codex/ folder still exists on disk with all contents intact (verify with ls)
.gitignore already contains .codex/ entry (confirm, do not duplicate)
No commit is created for this change alone — only stage the removal
acceptance.verification: Run: git ls-files .codex/ (expect empty); ls .codex/ (expect contents); grep '.codex' .gitignore (expect match)

subtasks.order_reason: Independent of other subtasks. Git hygiene must happen before any new commits to avoid re-tracking .codex/ files.

subtasks.title: Recreate qyweb_editing skill using skill_creator

artifact_quality: SKILL.md has YAML frontmatter with name: qyweb_editing (or name: "qyweb_editing") and a comprehensive multi-sentence description trigger
Skill follows the structural pattern of /Users/qy/.codex/skills/imagegen/SKILL.md: YAML frontmatter, decision tree or workflow section, conventions section, inline examples
Skill covers all 5 collections (_posts, _blogs, _figs, _shots, _vlogs) plus static pages (about.md, notes.html, etc.) plus sidebar navigation
Includes inline examples showing how to create each content type with sample front matter and expected filenames
Skill created via the skill_creator skill workflow (not manually written from scratch)
Old single-file skill deleted from all 3 locations before deploying new version

SKILL.md deployed and byte-identical (SHA256 match) across all 3 locations: /Users/qy/.claude/skills/qyweb_editing/SKILL.md, /Users/qy/.codex/skills/qyweb_editing/SKILL.md, and workflow backup location
Skill appears in Claude Code's active skill registry (verify with claude skill list or equivalent)
acceptance.verification: Run: shasum -a 256 on all 3 SKILL.md locations (expect identical hashes); head -5 SKILL.md (expect YAML frontmatter); grep -c '_posts\|_blogs\|_figs\|_shots\|_vlogs' SKILL.md (expect >= 5 matches)

subtasks.order_reason: Core deliverable. Must complete before subtask 003 can populate the references/ subfolder. Uses existing skill content as knowledge base for skill_creator.

subtasks.title: Create references/ subfolder with template and convention files

artifact_quality: references/ subfolder exists inside qyweb_editing skill folder at all 3 deployment locations
Contains at minimum 6 files: post-template.md, blog-template.md, fig-template.md, shot-template.md, vlog-template.md, quick-reference.md
Each template has valid YAML front matter (parseable by yaml.safe_load without errors) with the correct layout field for its collection type
Templates match site conventions: correct front matter field order, correct layout values (post, blog, fig, shot, vlog), correct permalink patterns
quick-reference.md covers all 5 collections with filename patterns, front matter fields, and asset path conventions
Optionally includes site-conventions.md (condensed, under 200 lines) summarizing full site architecture
All 3 references/ folders are byte-identical (verified with diff -r or checksums)
SKILL.md references the references/ folder with guidance on when to read each file
acceptance.verification: Run: ls references/ in each location (expect 6+ files); python3 -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open("post-template.md"))' for each template; diff -r across all 3 locations (expect no differences)

subtasks.order_reason: Depends on subtask 002 — the skill folder must exist before references/ can be populated. Templates are derived from reading actual site content files and the existing skill knowledge.
